concept.title: Extension Points and Plug-In Fundamentals
concept.shortdesc: DITA-OT plug-ins use extension points declared in plugin.xml to integrate changes into the toolkit without modifying core code.

section.title: plugin.xml is the contract
section.p: DITA-OT provides a series of extension points used to integrate changes into core code. Extension points are defined in plugin.xml; when plug-ins are installed, the toolkit makes those extensions visible to the rest of the toolkit.
DITA-OT explains that plugin.xml controls all aspects of a plug-in, using predefined extension points to locate changes and integrate them into DITA-OT.
Also important for teams: DITA-OT provides a RELAX NG schema and describes validating plugin.xml with an xml-model processing instruction.

ul.li: Plug-in IDs must be unique dot-delimited tokens (reverse-domain convention recommended).
Dependencies are declared with <require plugin="..."/> (critical for correct load order in XSLT and Ant integration).
